content.text: review this change

I’ll review this as a code review: first identify the active diff and branch shape, then trace the changed code paths enough to report concrete risks with file and line references. I’m also doing a quick memory check for prior `git-sync` context so I don’t miss known repo conventions.

The working tree is clean on `nodo/convert-sha256`, so I’m treating the branch delta as the change under review rather than uncommitted edits. Next I’m comparing it against the mainline ref and locating the PR metadata if it exists.

The branch adds a new `convert-sha256` command plus a substantial `sha256convert` package and tests. I’m going into the implementation and tests now, then I’ll run the targeted verification once I understand the intended behavior.

So far the core path is: smart-HTTP fetch into a temporary SHA1 bare repo, DFS-rewrite objects into a SHA256 repo, then write refs/notes/mapping/signing. I’m checking the edge cases around ref selection, HEAD, notes, gitlink handling, and real Git interoperability rather than only the unit-level translator behavior.

One thing I noticed while reading: the default tests cover the translator heavily, while the full smart-HTTP conversion and signing tests are behind `GITSYNC_E2E_SHA256_HTTP_BACKEND`. I’m going to run the focused package tests first, then decide whether the gated end-to-end test is worth running locally for the review.

The focused package tests and whitespace check pass. I’m going to run the gated local `git http-backend` conversion tests as well, because this feature depends on real Git accepting the SHA256 output, not just go-git decoding it.

content.text: The main issues I’m seeing are around the new command’s contract boundaries: it says branches/tags are always included, but it forwards the generic exclude-prefix filter into planner code that can drop them; and the side-output refs are not reserved from source refs. I’m doing one full test pass now so the verification section is current.

  1386	## Failures and how to do differently
  1387	
  1388	- Symptom: a proto comment or design note claims repo-scoped JWTs use `sub=account:<workload-name>` or another new subject family. Cause: ADR wording or speculative design got ahead of the runtime verifier and consumer path. Fix: verify `core/authn`, `core/model.ParseAccountID`, and `server/auth/interceptor.go` before approving any subject-model statement [Task 1]
  1389	- Symptom: a design discussion quietly turns a comment/docs fix into a cross-cutting auth migration. Cause: the proposed fix mixes current RPC surface work with a larger principal-namespace change. Fix: separate "comment/docs fix now" from "auth contract change later" and say explicitly when the broader design is still unsettled [Task 1]
  1390	- Symptom: a new verifier causes ordinary mirror Bearer traffic to fail with `ErrKeySourceUnavailable` / `503` instead of falling back to `gitAuth`. Cause: the service-token verifier was consulted before the code knew whether the bearer token was actually a service token. Fix: when a verifier is being used as a classifier, explicitly test unavailable-verifier cases and keep unrelated fallback credentials reachable [Task 2]
  1391	- Symptom: an auth review judges the contract in isolation. Cause: the change spans `entiredb` and `mirror-pipeline`. Fix: verify the adjacent repo’s current implementation before deciding whether the auth contract is wrong [Task 2][Task 3]
  1392	- Symptom: a review escalates into "mirror auth is broken" when the code path on the later branch is actually sound. Cause: lint-only failures and stale comments were mixed with runtime auth concerns from an earlier auth iteration. Fix: separate branch-specific runtime regressions from review hygiene/doc drift before escalating [Task 3]
  1393	- Symptom: `mise run lint` fails before meaningful lint results appear. Cause: environment restrictions block Go cache writes or golangci-lint schema/network fetches. Fix: classify that as environment noise first and rerun in a suitable environment instead of treating it as a code failure [Task 4]
  1394	- Symptom: lint is green but the branch diff changed unexpectedly. Cause: the repo lint task can run `gofix`. Fix: inspect the diff after lint and keep any extra edits explicitly accounted for [Task 4]

  1483	- when the user says only "can you review this change?" in `git-sync`, the right default is still a live branch/diff review of the active checkout rather than a repo-history summary or speculative concerns [Task 3]
  1484

content.text:
**Findings**

- P2: `--exclude-ref-prefix` can exclude branches and tags, despite the new command saying branches/tags are always converted. `Run` passes `ExcludeRefPrefixes` straight into `planner.BuildDesiredRefs` at [sha256convert.go](/Users/nodo/work/entire/git-sync/cmd/git-sync/internal/sha256convert/sha256convert.go:238), and the planner applies it to branch and tag selection too. That lets a user run a partial conversion and strand cross-branch message rewrites, which is exactly the invariant the command docs are trying to protect.

- P2: Side-output refs are not reserved from source refs. After writing source refs, the command unconditionally writes `refs/notes/sha1-origin` at [sha256convert.go](/Users/nodo/work/entire/git-sync/cmd/git-sync/internal/sha256convert/sha256convert.go:358), so a source repo converted with `--all-refs` that already has that notes ref gets it overwritten. Related: `--check` always skips `refs/notes/sha1-origin` and `refs/tags/converted/*` at [sha256convert.go](/Users/nodo/work/entire/git-sync/cmd/git-sync/internal/sha256convert/sha256convert.go:528), so legitimate source refs in those namespaces are miscounted or hidden.

- P3: Mixed flag/positional usage is broken for `--source-url <url> <target-dir>`. The positional target is only read from `args[1]` at [convert_sha256.go](/Users/nodo/work/entire/git-sync/cmd/git-sync/convert_sha256.go:43), so with `--source-url` set and one positional arg, the command errors instead of treating that arg as the target dir.

Verification run:
`go test ./cmd/git-sync/internal/sha256convert`, gated `GITSYNC_E2E_SHA256_HTTP_BACKEND=1 go test ./cmd/git-sync/internal/sha256convert -run 'TestRun_GitHTTPBackend' -count=1 -v`, `go test ./...`, `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`, and `mise run lint` all pass.

is it shippable?

No, I wouldn’t ship it as-is.

The tests and lint are green, including the gated local smart-HTTP conversion test, so the core conversion path looks healthy. But the two P2s are contract/data-safety issues:

1. `--exclude-ref-prefix` can violate the command’s “all branches and tags are always converted” guarantee.
2. `refs/notes/sha1-origin` and `refs/tags/converted/*` can collide with real source refs, and `--check` can hide those collisions.

The P3 positional-arg issue is not a release blocker by itself, but it is cheap to fix while touching this command. After fixing the two P2s and adding focused regression tests, I’d be comfortable calling it shippable.
fixed, can you re-review?

I’ll re-review the current head against the same concerns, then rerun focused verification. I’ll treat this as a fresh review of the branch, with extra attention on the previously flagged ref-scope and side-output collision fixes.

content.text: Verification found one CI-level issue: the full tests pass, but lint fails because `sha256convert_test.go` is not gofmt-formatted. I’m pulling the exact formatting diff so the finding can point at the concrete area.

**Findings**

- P2: `mise run lint` is still red because [sha256convert_test.go](/Users/nodo/work/entire/git-sync/cmd/git-sync/internal/sha256convert/sha256convert_test.go:1055) needs `gofmt`. The diff is just alignment in the `refs/notes/sha1-origin` test map, but this is CI-blocking.

- P3: The mixed flag/positional bug is still present in [convert_sha256.go](/Users/nodo/work/entire/git-sync/cmd/git-sync/convert_sha256.go:45). `--source-url <url> <target-dir>` still leaves `TargetDir` empty because the code only reads `args[1]`. I confirmed it with:
  `go run ./cmd/git-sync convert-sha256 --source-url http://example.invalid/repo.git /tmp/git-sync-review-out`
  It exits with `convert-sha256 requires a source URL and a target directory`.

The two earlier P2 functional concerns look fixed: branch/tag-dropping exclude prefixes are rejected before I/O, and side-output refs are now checked/skipped by exact written ref rather than broad namespace.

Verification:
`go test ./cmd/git-sync/internal/sha256convert`, `go test ./cmd/git-sync`, `go test ./...`, `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`, and the gated `GITSYNC_E2E_SHA256_HTTP_BACKEND=1 ... TestRun_GitHTTPBackend` pass. `mise run lint` fails on gofmt.

The gofmt issue is fixed, and the original mixed `--source-url <url> <target-dir>` path now has a helper and unit coverage. I found one adjacent CLI edge in that helper, so I’m confirming whether extra positionals are silently ignored or rejected.

content.text: The positional bug is fixed for the reported one-arg case, but the helper now leaves surplus positional args unconsumed; both the valid and extra-arg forms proceed to the source connection rather than rejecting the extra arg. I also found a staged change removing two `//nolint:ireturn` comments, so I’m rerunning lint against the exact staged/worktree state.

input.justification: Do you want to allow the gated sha256convert end-to-end test to bind a local httptest port?

content.text: The full test suite and gated end-to-end test pass, but lint is still red because the staged comment removal exposes the existing `ireturn` suppressions. I’m grabbing final line refs for the two remaining review points.

**Findings**

- P2: `mise run lint` is still red. The current index removes the two `//nolint:ireturn` suppressions above `openSource` and `normalizeAuth`, so golangci-lint reports `ireturn` at [sha256convert.go](/Users/nodo/work/entire/git-sync/cmd/git-sync/internal/sha256convert/sha256convert.go:705) and [sha256convert.go](/Users/nodo/work/entire/git-sync/cmd/git-sync/internal/sha256convert/sha256convert.go:739). `git status` shows this as a staged change: `M  cmd/git-sync/internal/sha256convert/sha256convert.go`.

- P3: The reported mixed `--source-url <url> <target-dir>` path is fixed, but the new parser silently ignores surplus positionals. In [convert_sha256.go](/Users/nodo/work/entire/git-sync/cmd/git-sync/convert_sha256.go:104), `positional` is consumed into unset fields, but leftover args are never rejected. Example: `--source-url <url> <target-dir> <ignored>` proceeds to source connection instead of erroring.

The previous gofmt issue is fixed, and the original positional case now has coverage.

Verification:
`go test ./cmd/git-sync -run TestResolveConvertSHA256Args -count=1`, `go test ./...`, `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`, and gated `GITSYNC_E2E_SHA256_HTTP_BACKEND=1 ... TestRun_GitHTTPBackend` pass. `mise run lint` fails on the staged `ireturn` suppression removal.
